purpose: Geometry-first machine-readable source layer. S1 should cover geometries themselves as broadly as possible, then attach theory-usage tags later. The main role is to identify what geometry it is so P-layer can later compile from it.

design_rule.core_definition: S1 stores normalized geometry source definitions in a geometry-first format, with theory usage attached as metadata.

field_notes.geom_id: Cross-layer stable geometry identity key shared with L/P/M.
field_notes.source_layer: Must be S1 for this schema.
field_notes.name: Human-readable geometry name.
field_notes.subtype: Specific normalized subtype such as euclidean_3d, minkowski, lorentzian_pseudo_riemannian, projective_hilbert, symplectic, etc.
field_notes.used_in_domains: Metadata tag listing where the geometry is used: relativity | quantum | cross_domain.
field_notes.usage_modes: How the geometry is used in those domains, e.g. core, approximation, computational, analytic, information.
field_notes.core_objects: Primary mathematical objects relevant to this geometry.
field_notes.core_relations: Primary relations/invariants/structures relevant to this geometry.
field_notes.required_operations: Operations needed so P-layer can eventually compile or manipulate this geometry.
field_notes.source_refs: References or provenance pointers for where this geometry definition comes from.
